disqualifiers: 87 files discovered during investigation suggests broad search surface
12 outstanding questions indicate UX decisions need clarification
Auth keyword present - even though using managed components, role-based access control and organization admin features are security-critical
Organization admin functionality (member management, role assignment) has compliance and security implications
Multiple stakeholder decisions needed (org visibility, switcher placement, admin controls scope)
reasons: Ticket scope is isolated to single page (/settings)
Uses existing Clerk pre-built components (no custom auth implementation)
No database schema or API changes required
All required components already available via @repo/auth/client

signals.riskNotes: Organization admin features (add/remove members, manage roles, configure 2FA requirements) are high-impact security features. While Clerk manages the underlying auth, ensuring proper role checks, correct component integration, and appropriate visibility boundaries requires careful planning and review. Questions around who can see organization settings, where controls should be placed, and what admin features are needed indicate this needs stakeholder alignment.
signals.summary: While the technical implementation appears straightforward (embedding Clerk components into a tabbed settings page), the presence of authentication/authorization concerns, organization admin features, and multiple open questions about UX and access control warrant full planning mode. The auth keyword is not a false positive here—organization member management, role-based access, and admin controls are security-critical features that benefit from critic review to ensure proper role checks, UI visibility rules, and security boundaries are maintained. The 4 substantive questions (Q-001 through Q-004) indicate clarification is needed on visibility rules, role management, component placement, and admin feature scope before implementation.

ticket:
In Account settings, expose all of the Clerk embeddable UI for account management. This includes:

1. OrgSelector - organization selector/switcher
2. Admin Org controls (for org admins):
   - Add/remove organization members
   - Enable/configure 2FA requirements
   - Manage org roles and permissions
3. Individual user settings:
   - Password reset
   - Profile management
   - Security settings (personal 2FA, sessions, etc.)
4. Special 'Admin' section that only appears for users with org admin roles, containing all administrative controls

The /settings page should be restructured to have clear sections for personal user settings and (when applicable) organization admin settings.
